data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Users perceive more aesthetically pleasing design as easier to use.
data.technical_spec: WhatsApp's minimalist design contributes to user satisfaction and ease of use.

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: The time required to move to a target area, such as a button on the screen, is a function of the distance to the target and its size.
data.technical_spec: Large buttons and touch targets make it easier for users to interact with the app.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Time to make a decision increases with the number and complexity of choices.
data.technical_spec: Limit on-screen choices to simplify interactions and reduce cognitive load.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time using other apps, so they prefer WhatsApp to work similarly for familiar experiences.
data.technical_spec: Consistent user interface elements that align with general smartphone app usage patterns.

data.title: Law of Prägnanz
data.description: People are innately predisposed to perceive complex images in their simplest form.
data.technical_spec: Icons and messaging interface are designed for clarity and simplicity.
